Backend calls Stripe API: Create PaymentIntent
Never expose the full PaymentIntent object. Only return client_secret and minimal metadata.
Client confirms payment using Stripe.js / SDK
Stripe automatically determines if 3DS/SCA is needed based on card issuer, region, and risk assessment.
Stripe.js handles the 3DS modal/redirect automatically. No additional frontend code needed if using Payment Element.
Log failure reason for analytics. Present user-friendly error message, not raw Stripe error.
Funds are captured. Safe to fulfill the order. Always verify via webhook before shipping.
Common for bank-based payment methods. Do NOT fulfill until webhook confirms success.
Show a pending state to the user. Poll order status or use WebSocket for real-time updates.
Your payment was declined. Please try a different payment method.

data.value: Refund amount is in the charge.refunded event. Update order status and notify customer.

data.insert: Disputes must be responded to within the deadline or the funds are automatically returned.

data.value: Use PaymentIntent ID to retrieve status on reconnection. Never create a new PaymentIntent — reuse the existing one.
Frontend: disable submit button immediately. Backend: use Stripe idempotency keys to prevent duplicate charges.
Webhook still fires — order is updated server-side
Payment may still succeed even if the user closes the browser. Webhook is the source of truth.
Webhooks are usually near-instant but can be delayed. Implement polling as a fallback for time-sensitive flows.
Stripe caches idempotent responses for 24 hours. Use a deterministic key derived from your order ID.
